channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A young gorilla rescued from aircraft hold recovers at Istanbul zoo
item.description: A young gorilla rescued from a plane’s cargo hold is recovering at an Istanbul zoo while wildlife officers consider returning him to his natural habitat.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This video company is positioning itself for a world without TikTok
item.description: As TikTok pleads with the U.S. Supreme Court to let it continue operating in the United States, one video company is taking advantage of the uncertainty around TikTok’s sister app.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: As Prime Video wraps up its third season doing NFL games with an AFC wild-card matchup between the Pittsburgh Steelers and Baltimore Ravens on Saturday night, the “Prime Vision” feed and use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the broadcasts continue to change the way football fans are able to analyz
item.dc:creator: Joe Reedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta cutting DEI team, programs in broad shift
item.description: Meta told employees Friday it will cut its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) team and roll back several related programs, marking another major shift in policy for the leading social media company.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: 4 in 10 companies plan to replace employees with AI, WEF says
item.description: Forty-one percent of companies intend to cut their workforce in the next five years as many tasks are automated with AI, according to the World Economic Forum (WEF) Future of Jobs Report 2025.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Disney, Warner Bros., Fox kill streamed sports joint venture
item.description: Three of the nation's largest broadcast media companies have scrapped plans to launch a joint direct-to-consumer streaming platform for live sports, the group announced Friday. Warner Bros. Discovery, Fox Corp.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Meta puts the ‘Dead Internet Theory’ into practice
item.description: Meta’s mission statement is to “build the future of human connection and the technology that makes it possible.” According to Meta, the future of human connection is basically humans connecting with AI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tableau certification guide: How to boost your data analytics skills
item.description: Data visualization platform Tableau is one of the most widely used tools in the rapidly growing business intelligence (BI) space, and individuals with skills in Tableau are in high demand.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cohere goes ‘North’ with agentic AI
item.description: Large language model (LLM) provider Cohere has unveiled its new agentic AI offering — North — a low-code platform that will allow enterprises to build and deploy agents across different business functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Flying taxis are on the horizon as aviation soars into a new frontier
item.description: Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation and nearby Wisk Aero, with ties to aerospace giant Boeing Co. and Google co-founder Larry Page, are at the forefront in the race to bring air taxis to market in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Preventing this insidious email forwarding scam that will drain your bank account
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says business email compromise scams are a serious threat, as shown by Teresa W.'s near loss of thousands of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top tech stealing the show at CES 2025
item.description: Big tech coming in 2025 includes solar umbrellas, AI TVs, smart earbuds and crazy robots. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his take on the wonders revealed at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI is losing money on its pricey ChatGPT Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, in a post on X, says the AI ​​company is currently losing money on its ChatGPT Pro subscription. “People are using it much more than we expected,” he wrote. The company introduced its ChatGPT Pro subscription in December.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Facebook to end fact-checking, boost political content
item.description: The company said automated moderation would focus on serious issues like terrorism and child sexual exploitation instead.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2025 ransomware predictions, trends, and how to prepare
item.description: Zscaler ThreatLabz research team has revealed critical insights and predictions on ransomware trends for 2025. The latest Ransomware Report uncovered a surge in sophisticated tactics and extortion attacks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hacked Chrome extensions put 2.6 million users at risk of data leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are embedding dangerous code in Chrome extensions to steal your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation
